caption: Regimental standards are now proper banners: a swallowtail pennant in the unit's team colour with a darker cloth fold and white rim, capped by a brass pole finial -- replacing the plain rectangle -- with each type's emblem unchanged on the cloth. An infantry regiment marches a short leg while archers stand opposite, so a standard reads above both a moving and a standing block. Render-only; close-up before/after stills are embedded in the PR description.
